2724	/// C: LClosure *luaY_parser(lua_State *L, ZIO *z, Mbuffer *buff, Dyndata *dyd,
2725	///                           const char *name, int firstchar)
2726	/// Top-level entry point: parses a chunk and returns the main LClosure.
2727	/// LUAI_FUNC visibility.
2728	///
2729	/// PORT NOTE: In C, returns `LClosure *` (a GC object). In Rust (Phase A),
2730	///   we return `Box<LuaProto>` since we don't have GcRef<LuaLClosure> ready.
2731	///   Phase B will wrap this in a proper LuaLClosure / GcRef.
2732	pub fn parse(
2733	    state: &mut LuaState,
2734	    // TODO(port): z: ZIO — from lua-lex / lua-vm
2735	    // TODO(port): buff: LexBuffer — from lua-lex
2736	    dyd: DynData,
2737	    name: &[u8],
2738	    firstchar: i32,
2739	) -> Result<Box<LuaProto>, LuaError> {
2740	    let mut lexstate = LexState {
2741	        current: firstchar,
2742	        linenumber: 1,
2743	        lastline: 1,
2744	        t: LexToken::default(),
2745	        lookahead: LexToken::default(),
2746	        fs: None,
2747	        dyd,
2748	        source: None, // TODO(port): intern `name` via state
2749	        envn: None,   // TODO(port): intern b"_ENV" via state
2750	    };
2751	
2752	    // C: cl = luaF_newLclosure(L, 1); setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)
2753	    // TODO(port): allocate LuaLClosure via state
2754	
2755	    // C: lexstate.h = luaH_new(L); sethvalue2s(L, L->top.p, lexstate.h); luaD_inctop(L)
2756	    // TODO(port): create scanner table via state
2757	
2758	    // C: funcstate.f = cl->p = luaF_newproto(L)
2759	    let main_proto = Box::new(LuaProto::placeholder());
2760	    let mut main_fs = FuncState {
2761	        f: main_proto,
2762	        prev: None,
2763	        bl: None,
2764	        pc: 0,
2765	        lasttarget: 0,
2766	        previousline: 0,
2767	        nk: 0,
2768	        np: 0,
2769	        nabslineinfo: 0,
2770	        firstlocal: 0,
2771	        firstlabel: 0,
2772	        ndebugvars: 0,
2773	        nactvar: 0,
2774	        nups: 0,
2775	        freereg: 0,
2776	        iwthabs: 0,
2777	        needclose: false,
2778	    };
2779	    // C: funcstate.f->source = luaS_new(L, name)
2780	    // TODO(port): main_fs.f.source = Some(state.intern_str(name));
2781	
2782	    // C: luaX_setinput(L, &lexstate, z, funcstate.f->source, firstchar)
2783	    // TODO(port): lua_lex::set_input(state, &mut lexstate, z, source, firstchar)?;
2784	
2785	    mainfunc(&mut lexstate, state, main_fs)?;
2786	
2787	    debug_assert!(lexstate.fs.is_none());
2788	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.actvar.is_empty());
2789	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.gt.is_empty());
2790	    debug_assert!(lexstate.dyd.label.is_empty());
2791	
2792	    // C: L->top.p-- — remove scanner's table
2793	    // TODO(port): state.pop();
2794	
2795	    // C: return cl — closure is on the stack, too
2796	    // TODO(port): wrap proto in LuaLClosure and return GcRef
2797	    Err(LuaError::syntax(format_args!("TODO(port): parse returns placeholder")))
2798	}

delta.text:
` (replaced the `todo!()` body at line 39).

**What `hook lua-parse::parse` now does:** `parse_
stub` returns `Err(LuaError::syntax(...))` with a message naming the chunk and explaining the par
ser isn't wired. The protected-parser pcall converts this to `
LuaStatus::Syntax`, which `lua_cli` then surfaces as a normal Lua syntax error instead of a

runtime panic.

**Why not a direct call:** `lua-parse` already depends on `lua-vm`, so importing `lua_parse::parse` from `
lua-vm::do_` would be a cyclic crate dependency. A clean wire-

## Report

**File modified:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:30-55` (replaced the `todo!()` body at line 39).

**What `hook lua-parse::parse` now does:** `parse_stub` returns `Err(LuaError::syntax(...))` with a message naming the chunk and explaining the parser isn't wired. The protected-parser pcall converts this to `LuaStatus::Syntax`, which `lua_cli` then surfaces as a normal Lua syntax error instead of a runtime panic.

**Why not a direct call:** `lua-parse` already depends on `lua-vm`, so importing `lua_parse::parse` from `lua-vm::do_` would be a cyclic crate dependency. A clean wire-up needs either moving `protected_parser`/`f_parser` into `lua-parse` or installing a parser hook on `GlobalState` from `lua-cli` — both larger than the single-todo!() scope. (And note `lua_parse::parse` itself currently returns `LuaError::syntax(...)` as a placeholder, so a real call would be functionally identical.) The PORT NOTE in the comment documents this and points to the Phase B reconcile.

**New todo!() exposed next:** None inside this function. The next iteration will likely surface whichever todo!() the runtime hits when handling/printing the `LuaStatus::Syntax` from `load()` in `lua-cli/src/main.rs`.

**Build status:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is **green** (warnings only — pre-existing unused-Result warnings).
